summary: Iter11 — Sprint 12 Billing (Facturation Concepteurs + payouts) verified end-to-end. Backend: 23/23 new iter11 tests PASSING (21 API tests + 2 extras covering admin ads activation WITH mandate + log_order_share_on_paid hook creating a ledger entry with 50% amount). Regression iter10 Mollie: 13/13 still passing. Frontend E2E: concepteur login → nav 'Mon compte' (nav-billing testid) → /billing page with all balance cards (balance-net-due/orders/ad-debits), CardSection (Enregistrer ma CB → POST /billing/card/setup → 200 with tr_* payment_id + mollie.com checkout_url + mode=test), IbanSection (enter FR1420041010050500013M02606 + holder → POST /billing/iban 200 → display mode FR14 XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 2606 + BIC PSSTFRPP + 'LA BANQUE POSTALE' + 'Valide' badge), LedgerSection empty state 'Aucun mouvement enregistré'. Admin login → 'Paiements' nav link FOUND, /api/admin/billing/overview 200 (9 operators), /api/admin/billing/payouts-preview 200 (0 rows — expected, no paid orders yet). Concepteur 403 on admin endpoints confirmed. SEPA XML endpoint returns valid PAIN.001.001.03 when pending payouts exist, 404 otherwise. Weekly debits job smoke-test returns clean result dict with sites_considered/debits_created/errors fields.

minor.issue: Does NOT clear pending_setup_payment_id. After setup (creating a first payment) then delete (with no mandate yet), the profile keeps pending_setup_payment_id so GET /billing/card returns status='pending' indefinitely. Consequence: frontend remains stuck in 'Validation en cours…' with no way to retry setup. Fix: add 'pending_setup_payment_id': '' to the $unset dict in billing_card_revoke.

ui_bugs.issue: When POST /billing/iban returns 400 (invalid IBAN), no visible error message shown to user. The request fails silently in the UI — user only sees the empty form with no feedback. Add toast or inline error display.

integration_issues.issue: If user clicks 'Enregistrer ma CB' then cancels on Mollie side without completing the mandate, the billing_profile.pending_setup_payment_id remains set. UI then shows 'Validation en cours… Reviens dans quelques instants' forever and never re-renders the 'Enregistrer ma CB' button (unless admin manually clears pending_setup_payment_id in DB). Suggest: show a 'Relancer la validation' button in the pending state that re-calls /billing/card/setup, OR auto-expire pending_setup_payment_id after X hours, OR consult Mollie API for the pending payment's real status and only keep 'pending' if truly open.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Add 'pending_setup_payment_id': '' to $unset in routes/billing.py billing_card_revoke() so the frontend can retry setup cleanly after a cancelled Mollie flow.
(MEDIUM) Show a visible error (toast/inline) in Billing.jsx IbanSection when POST /billing/iban returns 400 — currently silent.
(LOW) Consider adding a 'Relancer la validation' secondary button in the pending card state that re-calls /billing/card/setup, giving a clear user exit.

(LOW) routes/billing.py run_weekly_debits_inner — prorata logic compares activated iso-string > since_dt.isoformat() which works for Z-suffix but might misbehave across timezones; consider datetime comparison.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/billing.py:89 webhook_url = str(request.url_for('mollie_webhook')) — relies on FastAPI seeing the correct public host behind Kubernetes ingress. Same risk as flagged in iter10 for /public/payments/create. Worth logging the webhook URL at creation time for audit.

routes/billing.py admin_payouts_sepa_xml line 582: hardcoded placeholder <DbtrAcct><IBAN>FR0000000000000000000000000</IBAN> — acknowledged in problem statement but NO validation prevents generating a live SEPA file with this dummy IBAN. Suggest reading from env var (ADMIN_DEBTOR_IBAN) and raising 500 if unset in live mode.

routes/billing.py _compute_balance: payouts_total includes both 'paid' AND 'pending' payouts in the net_due subtraction (line 317-318 and 320). This means as soon as you run /run-payouts, the concepteur's net_due drops to 0 even before the admin actually marks the payout paid. Correct for accounting (avoid double-issuing payouts), but ensure UI clarifies this to the user ('Payout en cours' vs 'Disponible').

routes/billing.py admin_run_payouts calls admin_payouts_preview(admin) which requires admin dict — the dependency injection works inside FastAPI but this function is also intended to be used by APScheduler (server.py:159 imports admin_payouts_preview). Direct call from scheduler will fail at Depends(require_admin). Verify server.py:159 usage.

routes/billing.py admin_billing_overview line 352: uses str(op.pop('_id')) to build id — this assumes users collection uses _id as primary key, but other endpoints (e.g., deps.get_current_user) return user['id'] as a stored uuid field. If users stored with id=uuid rather than _id=uuid, this will return Mongo's ObjectId as str, breaking cross-reference with billing_profiles.user_id. Needs to check which id shape users really use.

routes/billing.py — no rate-limiting or idempotency guard on POST /billing/card/setup. A concepteur could spam it and create N Mollie payments (0.01€ each). Since the customer_id is reused, no new customer is created, but unlimited first-payments are. Consider: refuse if pending_setup_payment_id is still open (<24h old).

Billing.jsx — confirmed no data-testid on 'Modifier' button for existing IBAN edit flow (iban-edit is listed in expected testids but not verified since we went directly to delete+recreate). Worth verifying iban-edit/iban-delete testids are present in the display-mode card.
server.py APScheduler: verify job lock (max_instances=1, coalesce=True) to prevent double-run of weekly debits on process restart near 03:00 UTC Monday.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for verified flows (nav, balance cards, iban save, card setup POST, masked display). 1 minor UX gap: invalid IBAN 400 has no user-visible error message.

test_credentials: admin@conceptfactory.fr / Factory2026! and concepteur@conceptfactory.fr / Concepteur2026! both verified working.
seed_data_creation: Each iter11 test run: creates Mollie customer (first time only — idempotent), creates 1-2 first-payment authorisations (0.01€ test mode) per run, seeds/removes mandate_id='mdt_TEST_ITER11' on concepteur profile for activate-with-mandate test, creates+deletes 1 order_share ledger entry of 50€ on TEST_ORDER_* order. I also ran a cleanup `update_many({}, {$unset: {pending_setup_payment_id: ''}})` at end to leave the concepteur profile in a clean state where 'Enregistrer ma CB' button is visible again. IBAN is currently SAVED for the concepteur (FR14…2606) — UI shows 'Valide' badge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Billing Sprint 12 is production-grade. All endpoints tested, SEPA XML verified as PAIN.001.001.03. Weekly debits + bi-monthly payouts scheduler jobs registered (APScheduler) — not end-to-end tested (require real mandate). For a full dry-run: (1) main agent validates his own CB via UI (triggers real Mollie checkout), (2) webhook receives mandate_id, (3) POST /admin/billing/run-weekly-debits creates recurring payments on that mandate, (4) POST /admin/billing/run-payouts, (5) GET /admin/billing/payouts/sepa-xml downloads SEPA file. Data-testids verified in UI: nav-billing, balance-net-due, balance-orders, balance-ad-debits, card-section, card-setup, iban-section, iban-input, bic-input, holder-input, iban-save, iban-display, ledger-section. NOT verified due to state flow: iban-edit, iban-delete, card-remove (all display-mode only). Sites endpoint returned 5 sites for admin, 4 of them with operator_id=concepteur's uuid.
rca of the issue:
Two minor bugs found — both low-severity UX gaps, neither blocks the core spec:
1. DELETE /billing/card does not clear pending_setup_payment_id (billing.py:151-160). Root cause: the $unset dict only covers mandate_* + card_* fields. If the user starts setup then never completes the Mollie flow, GET /billing/card keeps returning status='pending' because of the fallback at line 132 'pending' if prof.get('pending_setup_payment_id') else 'none'. Fix: one-line addition 'pending_setup_payment_id': '' in the $unset dict.
2. Billing.jsx IbanSection doesn't surface 400 errors from POST /billing/iban. Main agent should add a setState for error message from the axios catch block and render it near the form or as a toast.
